target: Build native iOS + macOS apps at full parity (same UI + all functionality) with the Pebo Compose Desktop app.

conventions.deferred_meaning: Code is written/staged but verification is blocked by a missing toolchain (e.g. Xcode, JDK 21, CocoaPods). Deferred deps still unblock dependents; re-run with --retry-deferred in a full environment to flip them to done.
conventions.ready_rule: A unit is ready when status == pending and every dependency is in {done, deferred}.

prerequisites.full_xcode: xcodebuild -version (Command Line Tools alone is NOT enough for iOS SDK/simulator)
prerequisites.cocoapods: pod --version (only if the Xcode project integrates the framework via CocoaPods)

units.title: Verify Apple build toolchain (Xcode, JDK 21, simulator, CocoaPods)
units.acceptance: Report which of: full Xcode, JDK 21, iOS simulator, CocoaPods are present. If Xcode/simulator absent, note that iOS build/run units must be deferred. This unit is informational and never blocks others.
units.notes: Run: xcodebuild -version; /usr/libexec/java_home -V; xcrun simctl list devices available; pod --version. Mark done if it reports cleanly even when tools are missing.
units.result_note: JDK21 installed; desktopTest green. No Xcode/simctl/CocoaPods -> iOS build/run units will defer.

units.title: Add iOS (+ native macOS) Kotlin targets and Apple source sets to composeApp
units.acceptance: composeApp/build.gradle.kts declares iosX64/iosArm64/iosSimulatorArm64 (binaries.framework baseName="ComposeApp", isStatic=true). Add appleMain (parent of iosMain) source set hierarchy and a Darwin Ktor engine dep (ktor-client-darwin) for iosMain. Optionally add macosArm64/macosX64 behind evaluation. Add jvmToolchain(21) if the desktop build needs it. `./gradlew :composeApp:help` succeeds. iOS framework-link verification may be deferred if Xcode is missing.
units.notes: Add ktor-client-darwin to libs.versions.toml. appleMain depends on commonMain; iosMain/macosMain depend on appleMain. Do NOT remove the desktop target. Keep desktop build green.
units.result_note: iOS targets (x64/arm64/sim) + ktor-darwin added; :composeApp:help config verified. Desktop build unaffected.

units.title: Create iosApp Xcode project + MainViewController bridge
units.acceptance: An iosApp/ Xcode project exists (standard Compose Multiplatform layout) hosting the shared UI via a SwiftUI ComposeView wrapping the Kotlin `MainViewController()`. iosMain exposes `fun MainViewController(): UIViewController = ComposeUIViewController { App(...) }`. Framework links for iosSimulatorArm64 (defer if no Xcode).
units.notes: Use the JetBrains Compose MP iOS template structure: iosApp/iosApp.xcodeproj, iOSApp.swift, ContentView.swift, Info.plist. Framework name ComposeApp. App() needs a NotesViewModel + dataDir (see ios-entry-wiring).

units.title: Apple actuals for Platform.kt (newId, nowIso)
units.acceptance: appleMain provides `actual fun newId()` (NSUUID().UUIDString) and `actual fun nowIso()` returning a millisecond ISO-8601 UTC string matching the desktop format (Instant.truncatedTo(MILLIS).toString()). Unit-test format if practical.
units.notes: Desktop uses java.util.UUID + java.time.Instant. Match the exact ISO string shape (e.g. 2026-06-18T20:14:00.123Z). Consider kotlinx-datetime or NSISO8601DateFormatter with fractional seconds.
units.result_note: iOS newId (NSUUID) + nowIso (NSISO8601) written; needs Xcode to compile/verify.

units.title: Apple actuals for Pkce.kt (verifier + S256 challenge)
units.acceptance: appleMain provides `actual fun newPkceVerifier()` (cryptographically random, RFC 7636 charset) and `actual fun pkceChallengeS256(verifier)` (SHA-256 then base64url no-pad). Verify against a known RFC 7636 test vector in a shared test.
units.notes: Use SecRandomCopyBytes for randomness and CommonCrypto CC_SHA256 (or CryptoKit SHA256) for the digest. Match desktop Pkce.desktop.kt output exactly for the same verifier.
units.result_note: Challenge moved to shared okio SHA-256 + verified vs RFC 7636 vector (commonTest). iOS newPkceVerifier (SecRandomCopyBytes) written; needs Xcode to compile.

units.title: iOS actual for FolderPicker.pickFolder (UIDocumentPicker)
units.acceptance: iosMain provides `actual fun pickFolder(...)` presenting UIDocumentPickerViewController in folder mode, returning the selected directory path (security-scoped bookmark resolved) or null on cancel.
units.notes: iOS sandbox: a picked folder needs startAccessingSecurityScopedResource + bookmark persistence. Coordinate with filesystem-apple. May present from the key window's rootViewController.

units.title: macOS actual for FolderPicker.pickFolder (NSOpenPanel)
units.acceptance: Native-macOS source set provides `actual fun pickFolder(...)` using NSOpenPanel (canChooseDirectories=true, canChooseFiles=false). If the native macOS target is deferred, the JVM-desktop Swing picker already covers macOS — note that and defer.
units.notes: Only needed if pursuing the native macosArm64 Compose target. The JVM desktop target's FolderPicker.desktop.kt (Swing) already works on macOS.

units.title: Darwin Ktor engine wiring for Apple HTTP client
units.acceptance: HttpClientFactory builds a working client on Apple targets using the Darwin engine (desktop uses CIO). Sync/OAuth Ktor calls compile and run on iOS/macOS.
units.notes: Check commonMain/auth/HttpClientFactory.kt for how the engine is selected. Provide an appleMain engine (io.ktor:ktor-client-darwin).
units.result_note: ktor-client-darwin added to iosMain; HttpClientFactory auto-selects engine. Needs iOS build to verify.

units.title: Apple actuals for NoteExport (HTML/PDF/PNG/JPG/DOCX + save dialog)
units.acceptance: appleMain provides `actual fun exportNote(...)` for all five ExportFormat values and `actual fun pickSaveFile(...)`. iOS save -> share sheet / Files; macOS save -> NSSavePanel. Raster formats resolve relative image paths against attachmentsDir.
units.notes: Mirror NoteExport.desktop.kt. HTML is plain string output. PDF via PDFKit/CoreGraphics; PNG/JPG via UIGraphicsImageRenderer (iOS) / NSImage (macOS); DOCX reuse any shared OOXML builder the desktop uses. Large unit — split if needed.

units.title: Apple actual for MarkdownImageRenderer (raster export support)
units.acceptance: The image renderer used by raster export has an Apple implementation matching the desktop output closely enough that PNG/JPG export produces a correct note image.
units.notes: See desktopMain/export/MarkdownImageRenderer.desktop.kt for the contract.

units.title: Keychain-backed SecureTokenStore for iOS + macOS
units.acceptance: An appleMain KeychainSecureTokenStore implements SecureTokenStore (load/save/clear) using the Security framework (kSecClassGenericPassword). Round-trip save/load/clear verified by a test where the simulator is available (else deferred).
units.notes: Replaces the Windows-DPAPI DesktopSecureTokenStore on Apple. Also fixes the macOS-desktop gap (DesktopSecureTokenStore requires isWindows()).

units.title: ASWebAuthenticationSession OAuth sign-in for Apple
units.acceptance: Apple sign-in uses ASWebAuthenticationSession with a custom redirect scheme, returns the auth code to OAuthSessionManager, and persists tokens via the Keychain store. AppleOAuthClientIds resolves client IDs (env/Info.plist), gracefully disabled when unset (parity with desktop).
units.notes: Replaces DesktopOAuthRedirectReceiver (loopback HTTP) + DesktopOAuthSignIn. Register the redirect URL scheme in Info.plist.

units.title: Hoist welcome-note seeding to shared code
units.acceptance: seedWelcomeNoteIfNeeded (currently in desktop main.kt) is available to Apple entry points without duplication (moved to commonMain or appleMain). Desktop still uses it; behavior unchanged.
units.notes: Pure Okio/Note logic — safe to move to commonMain. Keeps iOS/macOS first-run identical to desktop.
units.result_note: Hoisted seedWelcomeNoteIfNeeded to commonMain; desktop rewired + green.

units.title: Notes directory + Okio FileSystem on Apple sandboxes
units.acceptance: A default notes base dir is chosen per platform (iOS: app Documents dir via NSSearchPathForDirectoriesInDomains; macOS: ~/Pebo or app container) and LocalNoteStore reads/writes there with Okio FileSystem.SYSTEM. Atomic writes work in the sandbox.
units.notes: Desktop uses System.getProperty(user.home)+"/Pebo". iOS must use the sandboxed Documents directory; iCloud Drive is a later enhancement.

units.title: Compose resources (fonts) load on Apple targets
units.acceptance: Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts and any composeResources load correctly on iOS/macOS (no missing-resource crashes). Typography matches desktop.
units.notes: compose.resources block already set (publicResClass). Verify resource accessors resolve on Apple.

units.title: iOS app initialization (prefs, store, ViewModel, App())
units.acceptance: MainViewController builds FileAppPreferences + LocalNoteStore in the iOS Documents dir, seeds the welcome note on first run, constructs NotesViewModel (with storeFactory), and renders App(vm, dataDir). The app launches in the simulator to the three-pane/compact UI (defer launch if no Xcode).
units.notes: Mirror desktop main.kt wiring. Use a CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob()+Dispatchers.Default).

units.title: macOS app via Compose Desktop (.dmg) — build, run, polish
units.acceptance: `./gradlew :composeApp:run` launches on macOS and `:composeApp:packageDmg` produces a branded Pebo.dmg. macOS-only crashers are removed (e.g. secure-token-store now works on macOS). App uses the .icns icon and a sensible window. Requires JDK 21.
units.notes: This is the pragmatic, fully-supported 'Mac app' — same codebase/UI as Windows desktop. main.kt already no-ops the Windows title-bar tweak on non-Windows.
units.result_note: Built .app + Pebo-1.0.0.dmg; launched & seeded ~/Pebo/notes; desktopTest green. macOS Keychain gap closed in DesktopSecureTokenStore.

units.title: (Stretch) Native macOS Compose target entry point
units.acceptance: If Compose Multiplatform 1.8.2 supports a native macosArm64 Compose UI window, provide a macosMain main() launching the App() in a native window. If unsupported/experimental-unstable, DEFER with a clear note and rely on macos-desktop-dmg.
units.notes: Compose-for-desktop on macOS runs on the JVM; native macOS Compose UI is experimental. Evaluate; do not block parity on it.

units.title: Parity: note list (hierarchy, pin, filters, selection)
units.acceptance: NoteList renders identically and works on iOS + macOS: parent/child hierarchy lines, pinned ordering, All/Untagged/Tag/Trash filters, selection drives the editor. Compact (iOS phone) layout shows list->editor navigation.
units.notes: ui/NoteList.kt. Compact layout already in App.kt CompactLayout.

units.title: Parity: Markdown editor (typing, autosave, live formatting)
units.acceptance: Editor typing, autosave, live Markdown rendering, word count, and the soft keyboard (iOS) behave correctly. richeditor-compose works on Apple.
units.notes: ui/Editor.kt, MarkdownEditing.kt, MarkdownVisualTransformation.kt.

units.title: Parity: editor toolbar actions
units.acceptance: Bold/italic/inline-code/headings/lists/task-lists/quote/link/code-fence toolbar actions rewrite raw Markdown correctly on iOS + macOS (round-trips preserved).
units.notes: ui/EditorToolbar.kt.

units.title: Parity: Markdown preview (all elements)
units.acceptance: Foldable headings, bold/italic/code/links, bullet/numbered/task lists, blockquotes, fenced code, tables, images, hr all render in the preview on Apple.
units.notes: ui/MarkdownPreview.kt.

units.title: Parity: Mermaid diagrams on Compose canvas
units.acceptance: Flowchart + sequence diagrams render on the Compose canvas on iOS + macOS, matching desktop.
units.notes: ui/Mermaid.kt — pure Compose canvas, should port directly.

units.title: Parity: code-fence syntax highlighting
units.acceptance: Fenced code blocks are syntax-highlighted identically on Apple.
units.notes: ui/SyntaxHighlight.kt.

units.title: Parity: outline folding + Focus mode
units.acceptance: Outline with heading folding works; Focus mode (centered canvas) toggles via Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+F (macOS) and a UI affordance (iOS). Esc/back exits.
units.notes: ui/Outline.kt; App.kt key handling. iOS needs a button since no keyboard shortcut.

units.title: Parity: sidebar nested tag tree + tag icons
units.acceptance: Nested #tag tree, 376 named tag icons, filter-by-tag, pin/untagged/trash all work on Apple. Sidebar is a drawer on iOS compact.
units.notes: ui/Sidebar.kt, TagIcons.kt, TagMeta.kt.

units.title: Parity: inline #tag autocomplete
units.acceptance: Typing # shows inline tag autocomplete on iOS + macOS.
units.notes: ui/TagComplete.kt.

units.title: Parity: command palette (Cmd/Ctrl+K)
units.acceptance: Command palette opens via Cmd/Ctrl+K (macOS) and a toolbar/FAB affordance (iOS), centers correctly, and runs all actions. Centering must not rely on Compose-Desktop-only window pixel APIs.
units.notes: ui/CommandPalette.kt has a Compose-Desktop HiDPI window-size comment — verify it works or adapt for Apple.

units.title: Parity: Settings + Storage provider screen
units.acceptance: Settings screen (theme mode, palette, storage backend) works on Apple. 'On this device' + 'Any folder you choose' use the Apple folder picker. Cloud backends show the same gated state as desktop.
units.notes: ui/SettingsScreen.kt, StorageProvider.kt.

units.title: Parity: 37 themes + Light/Dark/System
units.acceptance: All 37 palettes and Light/Dark/System switching render correctly on iOS + macOS. System mode follows the OS appearance.
units.notes: ui/theme/*. Verify isSystemInDarkTheme works on Apple.

units.title: Parity: export flow (HTML/PDF/DOCX/JPG/PNG)
units.acceptance: Exporting a note to each format works end-to-end on iOS (share sheet) and macOS (save panel), with relative images resolved.
units.notes: Ties the export actuals into the UI action.

units.title: Parity: note context menus (right-click / long-press)
units.acceptance: Open, pin/unpin, add child, move to Trash, restore, delete-permanently are reachable: right-click on macOS, long-press/overflow on iOS.
units.notes: Desktop uses right-click; iOS needs long-press or an overflow menu.

units.title: Shared tests green on Apple targets
units.acceptance: commonTest compiles for Apple; `./gradlew :composeApp:iosSimulatorArm64Test` (and/or macosArm64Test) passes. Add appleTest actuals where desktopTest had platform-specific tests. Defer the simulator run if Xcode is missing but ensure compilation.
units.notes: There are 19 commonTest + 7 desktopTest files. Port platform-specific desktop tests (DPAPI store, loopback receiver) to Apple equivalents or guard them.

units.title: Build + smoke-test the iOS app in the simulator
units.acceptance: `./gradlew :composeApp:linkDebugFrameworkIosSimulatorArm64` succeeds and the Xcode app builds and boots in an iOS simulator; smoke test create/edit/tag/export a note. DEFER if full Xcode/simulator is unavailable.
units.notes: xcodebuild -scheme iosApp -sdk iphonesimulator; xcrun simctl boot/install/launch.

units.title: Build + smoke-test the macOS app
units.acceptance: macOS app launches (JVM desktop run and/or native binary) and a smoke test (create/edit/tag/theme/export) passes; packageDmg artifact produced. Requires JDK 21.
units.notes: Pragmatic Mac deliverable is the Compose Desktop .dmg.

units.title: Update README + docs for iOS/macOS
units.acceptance: README reflects iOS + macOS targets: badges, prerequisites (Xcode, JDK 21), run/build instructions (iosApp scheme, gradle tasks), and updated project structure (appleMain/iosMain/iosApp).
units.notes: Roadmap already promises native iOS/macOS; move them to Features and document how to run.

units.title: (Optional) CI workflow building Apple targets
units.acceptance: A GitHub Actions workflow on a macos runner builds the Apple targets and runs shared tests. Skip/defer if the user does not want CI.
units.notes: Use macos-latest, JDK 21, Xcode select; cache Gradle + konan.
